response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/web developer with solid software craftsmanship skills, but the role requires deep embedded LAN-switch firmware expertise, native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, and Linux kernel modification experience — none of which appear in the resume. The C/C++ work at National Instruments was brief (~6 months) and unrelated to networking security or embedded firmware. Critical hard requirements (Japanese fluency, kernel-level Linux work, security/networking domain) are missing.
strengths: Long track record of professional software development across multiple stacks
Strong testing/TDD discipline and developer-tooling background
Some low-level C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and Linux familiarity
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency indicated (hard requirement)
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF/SNMP, or security-module experience
No Linux kernel source modification experience; C is not a primary or recent language
